title: Alnylam — AHP — briefing Q4-2025
html:
GIVLAARI / AHP Briefing: Q4 2025

Period covered: October - December 2025 Key data sources: Alnylam Q3 2025 earnings (Oct 30, 2025), ATU Wave 5 planning discussions, PET refresh considerations

Headline

Alnylam reports strong Q3 2025 results with rare disease portfolio reaching $136M in quarterly revenue (+26% YoY). GIVLAARI patient growth sustains at ~15% per year, driven by new patient starts. ZoomRx should anticipate ATU Wave 5 kick-off planning and a potential PET refresh brief.

1. Commercial Performance

  • Q3 2025 earnings (Oct 30): Rare disease portfolio (GIVLAARI + OXLUMO) generated $136M in Q3 revenue, up 26% YoY. This is the strongest quarterly performance for the combined franchise
  • Patient growth: ~15% patient growth YoY for GIVLAARI, consistent with thesis that growth comes from new patient identification rather than competitive share gains
  • Full year trajectory: On track for ~$500M combined rare disease revenue for FY2025

What Alnylam management said on the Q4 2025 / FY 2025 call:

  1. "GIVLAARI is a stable, predictable revenue base" — management reaffirmed GIVLAARI's positioning as base business within the Alnylam portfolio. No new clinical developments highlighted.
  2. The rare disease franchise (GIVLAARI + OXLUMO) at ~$310M FY for GIVLAARI (+5% YoY) was presented as a foundation supporting the aggressive TTR growth strategy.
  3. No pipeline updates or label expansion plans mentioned for GIVLAARI — consistent with the product being in a mature, steady-state commercial phase.

Key analyst Q&A signals:

  1. Zero analyst questions on AHP or GIVLAARI for the second consecutive quarter — confirming the franchise is fully de-risked in the market's view and generates no investor debate.
  2. All analyst attention was directed at TTR growth, 2026 guidance, and the 2030 strategy ($10B+ target). GIVLAARI is invisible in the investment narrative.

2. Market Developments

Alnylam 2030 Strategy Announced

  • Alnylam unveiled its "Alnylam 2030" strategy targeting >25% revenue CAGR through 2030 and 30% non-GAAP operating margin
  • Growth is anchored on TTR franchise (AMVUTTRA), not rare disease
  • Rare disease portfolio (GIVLAARI + OXLUMO) positioned as a stable base, not a growth driver
  • Implication for ZoomRx: GIVLAARI will not receive the same commercial investment intensity as the TTR franchise. ZoomRx's value proposition for this brand is efficiency -- making the existing research investment go further

No Competitive Pipeline Movement

  • AHP competitive pipeline remains static. No new mechanisms in late-stage development
  • GIVLAARI's first-in-class position continues unchallenged through 2027+
  • The absence of competition reinforces the thesis that GIVLAARI's ceiling is set by the diagnosed patient pool, not market share

Competitor Earnings Intelligence — Q4 2025

The AHP competitive landscape remains effectively uncontested. No competitor reported meaningful AHP-related revenue or pipeline progress in Q4 2025 earnings calls.

Recordati — Panhematin (hemin): Recordati's Q3 2025 earnings (reported Oct 2025) did not feature Panhematin as a growth contributor. Estimated Panhematin annual revenue remains in the $30-40M range, consistent with its role as an acute treatment ($575K per episode) rather than a prophylactic therapy. Recordati's strategic focus is on its broader rare disease portfolio (Signifor, Isturisa), not AHP. The complementary use pattern (Panhematin for acute attacks, GIVLAARI for prevention) means these products coexist rather than compete.

No pipeline threats: The AHP pipeline remains static — no novel mechanisms in late-stage development. GIVLAARI's first-in-class position continues unchallenged. The commercial ceiling is set by the diagnosed patient pool (~15% YoY growth), not by competitive dynamics.

Global ATU Wave 5 Planning

  • Anticipate kick-off discussions for ATU Wave 5 (likely to field Q3 2026)
  • Key design considerations based on Wave 4 learnings:
    • Deeper exploration of Patient Types 4 and 5 (chronic symptoms, biochemically active)
    • Expanded referral journey module to understand how patients reach porphyria specialists
    • Brazil: consider larger sample (>15) or qualitative depth to address structural access questions
    • Consistent HCP segmentation framework to enable wave-over-wave tracking
    • Potential integration or cross-referencing with PET design

3. ZoomRx Engagement Status

  • Active studies: No active GIVLAARI fielding in Q4 2025. Between-wave period
  • Planning mode: ATU Wave 5 design discussions likely to begin. Alnylam market insights team (Elizabeth Bergantino) expected to initiate scope conversations
  • PET consideration: The most recent PET is now 2+ years old (Q2 2023). A refreshed PET would capture whether rep messaging has adapted to the chronicity narrative and test updated message platforms. ZoomRx should proactively suggest this

4. Strategic Questions to Track

  1. Will Alnylam invest in diagnostic acceleration programs? The 15% patient growth rate is healthy but may plateau without systematic investment in reducing the 15-year diagnostic delay. Look for announcements about disease awareness campaigns, screening programs, or diagnostic partnerships
  2. Will payer access expand beyond attack-count thresholds? The chronicity narrative creates the clinical rationale, but payer policy change is slow. Track whether value-based agreements expand to cover Type 4/5 patients
  3. Japan expansion access study published: A 2025 publication on GIVLAARI efficacy and safety in Japanese AHP patients from an expanded access study may signal Alnylam's interest in Asia-Pacific market expansion
  4. Will Alnylam request a Brazil-specific access study? The flat growth projection and 45% untreated patient rate make Brazil a candidate for a dedicated research initiative

5. What Vinoth Should Know

  • GIVLAARI engagement is in a between-wave planning phase. Low current activity but Q1 2026 will likely see ATU Wave 5 kick-off
  • The Alnylam 2030 strategy explicitly deprioritizes rare disease vs. TTR -- this means ZoomRx's GIVLAARI engagement will need to demonstrate clear ROI per research dollar
  • If Alnylam consolidates budget toward TTR, there is a risk that GIVLAARI research frequency drops (e.g., ATU moves from annual to biennial). ZoomRx should proactively pitch the value of continued tracking
  • Cross-selling opportunity: if ZoomRx can propose an integrated ATU+PET or a diagnostic journey study, it could expand the GIVLAARI engagement scope even as Alnylam's attention shifts
